'''''UFC 314: Volkanovski vs. Lopes''''' was a mixed martial arts event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on April 12, 2025, at the [[Kaseya Center]] in [[Miami, Florida]], United States.<ref name="Miami">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-fight-card-date-location-miami|title=UFC 314 officially heads to Miami|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-09|access-date=2025-02-09}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's fourth visit to Miami and first since [[UFC 299]] in March 2024.<ref name="Miami"/>

A [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] bout for the vacant title between former champion [[Alexander Volkanovski]] and [[Diego Lopes (fighter)|Diego Lopes]] headlined the event. Current champion [[Ilia Topuria]] vacated the title in order to move up to lightweight.<ref>{{Cite web|title=UFC 314 headlined by Alexander Volkanovski vs. Diego Lopes title bout with Ilia Topuria vacating|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-fight-card-volkanovski-vs-lopes|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-19|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref>

Former three-time [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Lightweight World Championship|Bellator Lightweight World Champion]] (also former [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] challenger) [[Michael Chandler]] and [[Paddy Pimblett]] met in a five-round lightweight co-main event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-michael-chandler-vs-paddy-pimblett|title=UFC 314 adds Michael Chandler vs. Paddy Pimblett – and it's five rounds|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-20}}</ref>

A [[UFC Flyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alexandre Pantoja]] and former interim title challenger [[Kai Kara-France]] was rumored to take place at this event, but it never materialized.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-01-29|title=Report: Alexandre Pantoja vs. Kai Kara-France set for UFC 314 in Miami on April 12|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/ufc/alexandre-pantoja-vs-kara-france-ufc-314/|access-date=2025-01-29|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Harvey Leonard}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between former [[UFC Welterweight Championship]] challenger [[Gilbert Burns]] and [[Michael Morales (fighter)|Michael Morales]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.lowkickmma.com/gilbert-burns-fights-against-ko-star-ufc-314/|title=Report – Gilbert Burns books return fight against unbeaten KO star at UFC 314 in Miami|publisher=lowkickmma.com|author=Ross Markey|date=2025-01-09|access-date=2025-01-10}}</ref> However, the pairing was moved to [[UFC 315]] due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-315-fight-card-montreal-muhammad-della-maddalenna-shevchenko-fiorot|title=UFC 315 title fights revealed: Belal Muhammad, Valentina Shevchenko set to defend in Montreal|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-20}}</ref>

Former three-time [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Featherweight World Championship|Bellator Featherweight World Champion]] (also former Bellator Lightweight World Champion) [[Patrício Pitbull]] made his promotional debut in a featherweight bout against former interim UFC Featherweight Champion [[Yair Rodríguez]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-patricio-pitbull-freire-vs-yair-rodriguez-miami|title=UFC signs Patricio 'Pitbull' Freire, set to debut vs. Yair Rodriguez in Miami|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-20}}</ref>

Former [[The Ultimate Fighter|Ultimate Fighter]] contestant [[Bryce Mitchell]] also fought [[Jean Silva]] in a three round featherweight bout. At the time of the contest, Silva was on a twelve fight win streak, including no losses in the UFC. <ref>{{Cite web |last=Skarka |first=Michael |date=2025-04-10 |title=UFC 314: Volkanovski vs. Lopes {{!}} Saturday, April 12 |url=https://espnpressroom.com/us/press-releases/2025/04/ufc-314-volkanovski-vs-lopes-saturday-april-12/ |access-date=2025-09-27 |website=ESPN Press Room U.S. |language=en-US}}</ref>

A women's bantamweight bout between [[Nora Cornolle]] and Hailey Cowan was expected to take place at [[UFC 315]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-315-hailey-cowan-vs-nora-cornolle-montreal|title=UFC 315 targets Hailey Cowan vs. Nora Cornolle addition to Montreal lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Farah Hannoun|date=2025-02-16|access-date=2025-02-16}}</ref> However, the bout was moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/features/matchmaking-bulletin/ufc-new-fights-2025/|title=UFC matchmaking bulletin: new fights for Gaethje, Mitchell, Hill, Aldo, and more|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Harvey Leonard|date=2025-03-02|access-date=2025-03-03}}</ref> At the weigh-ins, Cornolle weighed in at 137.5 pounds, one and a half pounds over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and she was fined 20 percent of her purse which went to Cowan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/04/ufc-314-volkanovski-vs-lopes-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream|title=UFC 314 weigh-in results: Stacked card set for Miami with one miss|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2025-04-11|access-date=2025-04-11}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[Geoff Neal]] and [[Carlos Prates]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-fight-card-geoff-neal-vs-carlos-prates-miami|title=UFC 314 adds Geoff Neal vs. Carlos Prates banger to Miami|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-20}}</ref> However, Neal withdrew from the fight due to an injury and the bout was subsequently cancelled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/ufc-314-main-card-cancellation|title=UFC 314 hit by huge main card cancellation|publisher=si.com|author=Mathew Riddle|date=2025-03-29|access-date=2025-03-29}}</ref>

A featherweight bout between Roberto Romero and promotional newcomer Alberto Montes was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chihuahua native to the UFC cage; Roberto Romero will return on April 12|url=https://oem.com.mx/elheraldodechihuahua/deportes/chihuahuense-a-la-jaula-de-la-ufc-roberto-romero-regresara-este-12-de-abril-21560497|date=2025-02-06|access-date=2025-02-06|publisher=oem.com.mx|author=Daniel Coronado}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> However, Montes withdrew from the fight for unknown reasons and Romero was moved to [[UFC on ESPN: Machado Garry vs. Prates]] two weeks later.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/mma/article/ufc-schedule-fight-cards-start-times-odds-how-to-watch-212552711.html|title=UFC schedule, fight cards, start times, odds, how to watch UFC 314: Alexander Volkanovski vs. Diego Lopes|publisher=sports.yahoo.com|author=Uncrowned Staff|date=2025-04-06|access-date=2025-04-06}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former two-time [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship|UFC Women's Bantamweight]] and one-time [[UFC Women's Featherweight Championship|Women's Featherweight Champion]] [[Amanda Nunes]] was announced as the next "modern wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas in June.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/04/ufc-hall-of-fame-amanda-nunes-induction-announcement|title=Amanda Nunes to be inducted into the UFC Hall of Fame|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-04-12|access-date=2025-04-12}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/4/13/24406358/bonuses-jean-silva-picks-up-extra-50k-for-dog-walking-bryce-mitchell-ufc-314-espn-mma-miami|title=Bonuses! Jean Silva cashes extra $50k for dog walking Bryce Mitchell - UFC 314|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Andrew Richardson|date=2025-04-13|access-date=2025-04-13}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Alexander Volkanovski vs. Diego Lopes'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Paddy Pimblett''' and '''Jean Silva'''
